name: Alabama
uniqueRequirements: Stage I learner permit begins at age 15 and requires supervised driving with a parent, guardian, instructor, or licensed driver age 21 or older in the front seat
Before a restricted Stage II license, the teen generally must hold the learner permit for at least 6 months and complete 50 hours of behind-the-wheel practice or an approved driver education course
Stage II drivers generally may not drive between midnight and 6 AM unless a listed exception or qualified adult applies
Stage II drivers generally may not carry more than 1 nonfamily passenger and may not use a handheld communication device while driving
ALEA public materials reviewed were more explicit about GDL rules than the exact scored written-test format, so the app uses a best-fit 30-question, 80% study format

name: Alaska
uniqueRequirements: Instruction permit available at age 14 and generally valid for 2 years
Drivers age 16 or 17 must hold the instruction permit for at least 6 months before licensing
At least 40 hours of supervised driving required, including 10 hours in progressively challenging conditions such as night driving or bad weather
Teen provisional drivers generally may not drive between 1 AM and 5 AM unless accompanied by a qualified adult or traveling for a permitted reason
Teen provisional drivers generally may not carry passengers unless accompanied by a parent, guardian, or qualified adult, with a limited sibling exception

name: Arizona
uniqueRequirements: Must hold permit for 6 months before road test
30 hours of supervised driving required (10 at night)
Drivers under 18 prohibited from driving 12am–5am for first 6 months of license
No passengers under 18 (except immediate family) for first 6 months of license

name: Arkansas
uniqueRequirements: Instruction permit available at age 14 and generally valid for 2 years; the current manual says it cannot be renewed
Permit holders must drive with a licensed driver age 21 or older seated beside them
Intermediate license holders are restricted from 11 PM to 4 AM unless an exception or qualified adult applies
Intermediate drivers generally may not carry more than 1 unrelated minor passenger unless a licensed driver age 21 or older is in the front seat
Arkansas materials reviewed were clearer on GDL restrictions than on scored-test length; the app uses the commonly cited 25-question, 80% format

name: California
uniqueRequirements: Minors (under 18) cannot use cell phones (even hands-free) while driving
Provisional license holders cannot drive between 11 PM and 5 AM for the first 12 months
Provisional license holders cannot transport passengers under 20 years old for the first 12 months without supervision
New 2025 'Daylighting' Law: No parking within 20 feet of the vehicle approach side of a crosswalk
New 2025 Insurance Minimums: $30k/$60k/$15k liability coverage required

name: Colorado
uniqueRequirements: Minor permit available at age 15; permit validity is generally 3 years
Colorado allows an at-home written test and publishes a 60-minute time limit for that knowledge test
Minor drivers generally must hold the permit for 12 months and complete 50 supervised hours including 10 at night before licensing
Licensed minors face midnight-to-5 AM curfew rules and passenger limits during the first year
Colorado pages reviewed were explicit about the 60-minute knowledge-test timing but less explicit about scored question count; the app uses the widely cited 25-question, 80% format

name: Connecticut
uniqueRequirements: Learner permit available at age 16 and knowledge test requires 20 correct out of 25
Teens generally need 30 hours of classroom instruction plus at least 40 hours behind the wheel
Road test wait is generally 120 days with approved school training or 180 days otherwise
First 6 months after licensing: no passengers except certain family or instructors; second 6 months: only immediate family passengers
Drivers under 18 may not use any cell phone or mobile electronic device, including hands-free, while driving

name: Delaware
uniqueRequirements: Delaware Level One learner permit starts at age 16
Sponsor must certify 50 supervised hours including 10 hours at night
First 6 months require full supervision; next 6 months allow unsupervised driving only from 6 AM to 10 PM
Between 10 PM and 6 AM, unsupervised travel is limited to specific exceptions such as school, work, or church activities
Delaware GDL drivers may not use cell phones or electronic devices, including hands-free, while driving

name: District of Columbia
uniqueRequirements: DC learner permit available at age 16 and generally valid for 2 years
For teen applicants in the GRAD program, the official practice test describes a 30-question knowledge test with a 75-minute limit
Permit driving is limited to 6 AM through 9 PM
Before a provisional license, the learner generally must hold the permit 6 months and complete a 40-hour certification log with a qualified adult age 21 or older
The exact GRAD passing threshold is less explicit on DC public pages reviewed, so the app uses the widely cited 80% standard (24 of 30)

name: Florida
agencyLong: Department of Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles
uniqueRequirements: Applicants under 18 can take the Class E Knowledge Exam online
Applicants under 18 must complete the 6-hour Driver Education Traffic Safety (DETS) course (replaced TLSAE in Aug 2025)
Permit holders must be accompanied by a licensed driver 21+ in the front passenger seat
For the first 3 months, driving is restricted to daylight hours only; after 3 months, until 10 PM

name: Georgia
uniqueRequirements: Test is strictly divided: 20 Road Signs and 20 Road Rules
Must score 75% (15/20) on EACH section independently to pass; failing one section fails the entire exam
Joshua's Law (30 hours class + 6 hours drive) is required to upgrade to a Class D license at age 16, not to get the permit at 15
New 2025 Law: 'Squatted' trucks (front fender raised 4+ inches higher than rear) are prohibited on public roads
New 2025 Law: Spectators at street racing events can now face criminal misdemeanor charges

name: Hawaii
uniqueRequirements: Learner permit testing begins at age 15 years and 6 months
Hawaii's statewide online learner-permit page says the permit knowledge test has 25 questions and a 1-hour time limit
Permit must generally be held for 180 days before the road test
Applicants under 18 generally must complete both driver education and behind-the-wheel certificates before the road test
Some county pages for other applicant types still mention 30-question written testing, so the app follows the current statewide online permit page for teen permit prep

name: Idaho
uniqueRequirements: Driver Training Permit begins at age 14 years and 6 months for applicants under 17; a Class D instruction permit is also available for applicants age 17 or older
Idaho's Class D knowledge test has 40 questions and allows up to 6 missed answers, which means 34 correct to pass
Drivers under 17 generally must complete a violation-free 6-month supervised instruction period with 50 hours of practice including 10 at night
During the supervised period, a licensed driver age 21 or older must sit in the front seat and all occupants must wear seat belts

Drivers under 16 are limited to daylight-only travel unless accompanied by a licensed driver age 21 or older, and newly licensed drivers under 17 face a 1-passenger-under-17 limit during the first 6 months

name: Illinois
uniqueRequirements: Permit phase requires 50 hours of practice (10 at night)
Must be enrolled in an approved Driver Education course if under age 17 and 3 months
Strict 'Scott's Law' (Move Over Law) enforcement
Dutch Reach method recommended for opening doors

name: Indiana
uniqueRequirements: Must hold learner's permit for 180 days before road test
50 hours of supervised driving required (10 at night)
Intermediate license holders prohibited from driving 10pm–5am for first year
No more than 1 passenger under 21 (except immediate family) for first 180 days of intermediate license

name: Iowa
uniqueRequirements: Instruction permit begins at age 14 and requires passing the knowledge and vision tests
Iowa's permit knowledge test uses 25 questions and an 80% passing score
Permit and intermediate drivers under 18 may not use a cell phone or other electronic communication or entertainment device while driving
Intermediate license generally requires 12 total months of permit time and allows unsupervised driving only from 5 AM to 12:30 AM unless a waiver applies
During the first 6 months of an intermediate license, Iowa may limit unsupervised teen drivers to no more than 1 unrelated minor passenger if the passenger restriction is accepted

name: Kansas
uniqueRequirements: Instruction permit begins at age 14 and is generally valid for 1 year
Kansas says the knowledge test uses 25 written questions on traffic laws and road signs and is not timed
Instruction permit driving requires a licensed adult age 21 or older with at least 1 year of driving experience in the front seat
Kansas teen licensing builds through restricted stages with 50 supervised hours including 10 at night before the less-restricted teen stage and no wireless device use while driving
For 16-year-old driving privileges, Kansas generally limits teens to 1 non-immediate-family minor passenger and allows independent driving only during listed times or trips

name: Kentucky
uniqueRequirements: Kentucky teen permit applicants may begin at age 15 and must pass written and vision tests through Kentucky State Police
Kentucky's written test is officially scored at 80%; public KSP materials indicate the operator permit exam uses 40 questions
Permit holders age 15 through 20 generally must hold the permit for 180 days before road testing
Before the intermediate license, the teen must complete a 60-hour practice log including 10 hours at night
Permit holders must drive with a licensed driver age 21 or older in the passenger seat, and public-school-age applicants 15 to 17 are also subject to Kentucky's school-compliance rule

name: Louisiana
uniqueRequirements: Louisiana's Temporary Instructional Permit (TIP) can begin at age 14, while the learner permit itself begins at age 15
The learner permit knowledge test has 40 questions and requires a minimum 80% score

Learner permit holders generally must complete 50 supervised hours including 15 at night and may drive only with a licensed parent, guardian, qualifying adult age 21 or older, or licensed sibling age 18 or older
Learner permits generally must be held 180 days before the intermediate license at age 16
Louisiana intermediate drivers face 11 PM to 5 AM curfew limits, a 1 under-21 non-family passenger rule during evening and overnight hours, and a no-cell-phone rule for younger drivers

name: Maine
uniqueRequirements: Maine's knowledge test has 30 questions and requires at least 24 correct answers
Applicants under 21 generally must hold the learner permit 6 months before road testing
Before a road test, applicants under 21 generally need 70 supervised hours including 10 at night with a licensed driver age 20 or older who has held a valid license for 2 years

For the first 270 days after licensing, drivers under 18 may not drive between midnight and 5 AM, may not use a mobile phone or handheld electronic device, and may not carry non-family passengers unless accompanied by a qualified adult
Maine's learner permit is generally valid for 2 years

name: Maryland
uniqueRequirements: Must hold learner's permit for at least 9 months before applying for a provisional license
60 hours of supervised driving required (at least 15 at night)
Provisional license holders may not drive midnight to 5 a.m. (exceptions for employment with written parental consent or medical emergencies)
First 5 months of provisional license: no more than 1 passenger under age 18, excluding immediate family
Zero tolerance BAC for drivers under 21 (0.02% threshold)

name: Massachusetts
uniqueRequirements: Parent/guardian must attend 2-hour driver education session
Must hold permit for 6 months before road test
40 hours supervised driving required

name: Michigan
uniqueRequirements: Level 1 License requires Segment 1 Driver Ed.
Kelsey's Law prohibits mobile phone use for Level 1 & 2 drivers.
Move Over Law includes utility and maintenance vehicles.
Zero Tolerance for BAC > 0.02% for under 21.

name: Minnesota
uniqueRequirements: Instruction permit begins at age 15 for teen drivers

Before a provisional license, teens generally must hold the permit 6 months and complete 50 supervised hours including 15 at night, or 40 hours including 15 at night if a parent completes the approved awareness class
For the first 6 months of a provisional license, nighttime driving is restricted from midnight to 5 AM and passengers under 20 are generally limited to 1 unless a parent or guardian is present
For the second 6 months of a provisional license, the under-20 passenger limit generally increases to 3 without a parent or guardian present
Minnesota public sources reviewed were explicit about teen GDL rules but less explicit about the exact scored question count, so the app uses a best-fit 40-question, 80% study format

name: Mississippi
uniqueRequirements: Mississippi's regular learner permit begins at age 15 and requires proof of school enrollment or other qualifying school-status documentation for most applicants under 18
Applicants must pass both the knowledge and eye exam, and the regular learner permit is generally valid for 2 years
A 16-year-old generally must hold the learner permit for 12 months or until the 17th birthday before qualifying for a regular Class R license
Applicants age 17 and older may obtain the learner permit and driver license on the same day after passing the knowledge and eye exam and presenting required documents

Mississippi public materials reviewed were much clearer about permit-age, school-status, and holding-period rules than the exact scored question count, so the app uses a best-fit 30-question, 80% study format

name: Missouri
uniqueRequirements: Must hold instruction permit for 182 days before applying for intermediate license
40 hours of supervised driving required, including 10 hours at night
Intermediate license holders under 18 may not drive alone between 1:00 a.m. and 5:00 a.m. except for school, work, emergencies, or with a licensed driver age 21 or older
Passenger restriction: no more than 1 non-family passenger under 19 during first 6 months of intermediate license
After the first 6 months of intermediate license, passenger restriction changes to no more than 3 non-family passengers under 19

name: Montana
uniqueRequirements: Montana teens may begin the learner-license path at age 14 1/2 while participating in or after completing state-approved traffic education; otherwise the learner path begins later depending on driver education status
The learner license is issued when the primary written test is passed, requires a vision test, and is generally valid for 1 year
Drivers under 18 generally must hold the learner license for at least 6 consecutive months and complete 50 supervised hours, including 10 at night, before the first-year restricted license
Montana's first-year restricted license generally bars driving from 11 PM to 5 AM except for listed exceptions and limits unrelated passengers under 18 during the first year
Montana public materials reviewed were more explicit about teen GDL rules than the exact scored written-test format, so the app uses a best-fit 25-question, 80% study format

name: Nebraska
uniqueRequirements: Nebraska's general Learner's Permit (LPD) begins at age 15 with a licensed driver age 21 or older in the seat beside the permit holder, while school-related permits can begin younger
Nebraska officially says the Class O written test uses 25 questions and applicants must answer at least 80% correctly to pass

Before the Provisional Operator's Permit (POP), teens generally must hold a permit for 6 months and complete driver safety school or present a 50-hour certification form with 10 hours between sunset and sunrise
The POP allows unsupervised driving from 6 AM to 12 midnight, with later driving limited to work, school activity, or adult-supervised exceptions
Nebraska bars interactive wireless communication device use for LPE, SCP, LPD, and POP holders, and the first 6 months of a POP generally limit the driver to 1 non-immediate-family passenger under 19

name: Nevada
uniqueRequirements: Nevada residents can apply for an instruction permit at age 15 1/2
Drivers under 18 must hold an instruction permit for at least 6 months before licensing
50 hours of supervised driving required before the road test (10 at night)
For the first 6 months after licensing, drivers under 18 may not carry passengers under 18 except immediate family members
Drivers under 18 may not drive between 10 PM and 5 AM unless traveling to or from a scheduled event
Applicants under 18 must submit a DMV-301 Certification of Attendance form
KnowToDrive Nevada allows a 60-minute online knowledge test; in-person DMV office testing has no time limit

name: New Hampshire
uniqueRequirements: New Hampshire does not issue a traditional learner permit for supervised practice driving
Drivers under 18 must complete a state-approved driver education program before licensing
Drivers under 18 must complete 40 hours of supervised driving (10 at night)

name: New Jersey
uniqueRequirements: Kyleigh's Law: Drivers under 21 with a permit or probationary license must display red reflectorized decals on the top left corner of both license plates
GDL curfew: No driving between 11:01 PM and 5:00 AM (Under 21)
New 2025 Requirement: Under-21 permit holders must complete 50 hours of supervised practice driving (10 at night) before getting a probationary license
6 Point ID Verification required for all licenses

name: New Mexico
uniqueRequirements: New Mexico's instructional permit begins at age 15 and requires the knowledge and vision tests, plus parental consent for applicants under 18
Teen applicants generally must be enrolled in an approved driver education course that includes DWI education and practice driving
Before a provisional license, the teen must generally hold the permit 6 months, complete 50 practice hours including 10 at night, and avoid a traffic conviction in the prior 90 days

New Mexico provisional drivers may not drive from midnight to 5 AM except for listed exceptions and generally may not carry more than 1 under-21 passenger who is not immediate family unless supervised by a licensed driver age 21 or older

Public New Mexico materials reviewed clearly state the 70% written-test passing standard but are less explicit about the scored question count for first-time teen testing, so the app uses a best-fit 25-question study format

name: New York
uniqueRequirements: Must correctly answer at least 14 of the 20 questions to pass
CRITICAL: You MUST correctly answer at least 2 of the 4 road sign questions to pass; failing this section causes a test failure regardless of total score
Junior Permit Restrictions: In NYC (5 Boroughs), you may ONLY drive between 5 AM and 9 PM and ONLY with a parent, guardian, or driving instructor (vehicle must have dual controls)
Junior License Restrictions: Holders of a Class DJ (Junior) License cannot drive in NYC under any circumstances

name: North Carolina
uniqueRequirements: The test is strictly split: You must pass a 'Regulatory Signs' recognition test (identifying signs by shape/color only) BEFORE you can take the 25-question written knowledge test
Level 1 Limited Learner Permit holders can only drive between 5 AM and 9 PM for the first 6 months
Under the 'Fender Bender' law, you must move your vehicle to the shoulder if involved in a minor crash with no injuries
Must have a 'Driving Eligibility Certificate' from your school to prove you are passing your classes

name: North Dakota
uniqueRequirements: North Dakota's learner permit begins at age 14 and requires the knowledge test, vision screen, and a sponsor's signature for applicants under 18
Permit holders ages 14-15 generally must hold the permit 12 months or until age 16, but at least 6 months; ages 16-17 generally must hold it at least 6 months or until age 18
Drivers under 16 generally must complete 50 supervised practice hours in variable conditions before licensing
North Dakota residents can qualify for a restricted license at age 15, and that license generally bars driving from sunset or 9 PM, whichever is later, until 5 AM except for listed reasons
Drivers under 18 in North Dakota may not use electronic communication devices while driving, including while operating on a learner permit

name: Ohio
uniqueRequirements: The test is cumulative: You must answer 30 out of 40 questions correctly (75%) to pass
Drivers under 18 holding a probationary license for less than 12 months cannot drive between midnight and 6 AM
Temporary Instruction Permit Identification Card (TIPIC) holders under 18 must practice for 50 hours (10 at night) before the road test
During the first 12 months of a probationary license, you may not drive with more than one non-family member passenger

name: Oklahoma
uniqueRequirements: Oklahoma's written exam is officially scored at 15 correct answers out of 20 questions
A learner permit generally begins at age 15 for applicants enrolled in approved driver education, or at age 16 without driver education
Learner permit holders generally may drive from 5 AM to 10 PM with a licensed driver age 21 or older and must hold the permit at least 180 days before the intermediate step
Before the drive test, teens generally must complete 50 behind-the-wheel hours including 10 at night, have no traffic convictions, and complete the Work Zone Safe online course

Oklahoma's under-18 graduated-license restriction generally allows driving from 5 AM to 10 PM, limits the teen to 1 passenger or household passengers, and requires a licensed driver age 21 or older in the front seat for broader exceptions

name: Oregon
uniqueRequirements: Oregon officially says the Class C knowledge test has 35 multiple-choice questions and requires 28 correct answers to pass
Applicants under 18 generally must hold the instruction permit for at least 6 months, and out-of-state permit time can count
Teens under 18 generally need 100 supervised hours without approved driver education or 50 supervised hours with an ODOT-approved course certificate
For the first year of provisional licensing, Oregon generally restricts driving between midnight and 5 AM except for listed exceptions and limits passengers under 20 in two stages
Oregon also makes mobile-device use unlawful while operating a motor vehicle, including talking or texting on a cell phone even with a hands-free accessory

name: Pennsylvania
uniqueRequirements: Must successfully answer 15 of the 18 questions to pass (83%)
Permit is valid for one year from the date of issue
Must complete 65 hours of supervised practice (including 10 at night and 5 in bad weather) before road test
Junior license holders cannot drive between 11 PM and 5 AM
Mandatory physical examination (Form DL-180) must be completed by a doctor/nurse practitioner before applying

name: Rhode Island
uniqueRequirements: Must hold learner's permit for 6 months before applying for a restricted license
50 hours of supervised driving required (10 at night)
During first 12 months of restricted license: no driving between 1 AM and 5 AM
During first 12 months of restricted license: no more than one non-family passenger under 21
Drivers under 18 prohibited from using any mobile device, including hands-free, while driving

name: South Carolina
uniqueRequirements: South Carolina's beginner permit begins at age 15 and requires the vision and knowledge tests

A regular beginner permit generally allows practice driving from 6 AM to midnight with a licensed driver age 21 or older who has at least 1 year of driving experience, and after midnight with a qualified adult
Teen drivers generally must hold the beginner permit more than 180 days, complete driver education, and log 40 practice hours including 10 at night before the teen-license stages

Conditional and special restricted teen licenses generally limit the driver to no more than 2 passengers under 21 unless a qualified adult is present, and use staged day, evening, and overnight supervision rules
South Carolina public materials reviewed were clearer about teen GDL steps and hour requirements than the exact scored written-test count, so the app uses a best-fit 30-question, 80% study format

name: South Dakota
uniqueRequirements: South Dakota's instruction permit begins at age 14 and generally requires the knowledge test unless a qualifying state-approved driver education course waives that testing step
Teens generally must hold the instruction permit 275 days without driver education or 180 days with approved driver education before the restricted step
Before a restricted permit, South Dakota generally requires 50 parent- or guardian-supervised hours including 10 at night and 10 in inclement weather
The restricted permit generally allows solo driving from 6 AM to 10 PM, requires a legal guardian for 10 PM to 6 AM driving, and uses staged passenger limits outside the immediate family or household

South Dakota officially states the knowledge test must be passed with a score of 80% or higher; public materials reviewed were less explicit about the scored question count, so the app uses a best-fit 25-question study format

name: Tennessee
agencyLong: Department of Safety and Homeland Security
uniqueRequirements: Must hold permit for 180 days (6 months) before road test
50 hours of supervised driving required (10 at night)
Intermediate license holders under 17 prohibited from driving 11pm–6am
No more than 1 passenger under 21 (except immediate family) for first 6 months of intermediate license

name: Texas
uniqueRequirements: The test is strictly split into two sections: Highway Signs (15 questions) and Traffic Laws (15 questions)
You must score at least 70% to pass the exam
Minors (under 21) face Zero Tolerance laws: Any detectable amount of alcohol results in license suspension
Provisional license holders (under 18) cannot drive between midnight and 5:00 AM
Provisional license holders cannot have more than one passenger under 21 who is not a family member

name: Utah
uniqueRequirements: Utah's learner permit begins at age 15 and requires passing a 50-question closed-book written knowledge test
Utah's 2026 driver handbook says the written knowledge test typically takes 30 to 45 minutes and requires a score of 80% or better, while learner-permit scores and permits remain valid for 18 months

For teen first-time licensing, Utah generally requires the permit to be held 6 months and until age 16, plus driver education, 40 practice hours with 10 after sunset, and the Traffic Safety and Trends Exam

Newly licensed Utah drivers ages 16-17 may not drive between midnight and 5 AM except for listed exceptions and generally may carry only immediate family members during the first 6 months or until age 18

Utah allows the permit-holding period to count from an out-of-state learner permit and permits several types of qualified front-seat supervisors, including a licensed parent, guardian, approved instructor, responsible adult signer, or an authorized adult age 21 or older

name: Vermont
uniqueRequirements: Vermont officially says the learner permit test is online, has 20 questions, and requires 16 correct answers to pass
A Vermont resident must be at least 15 years old to take the learner permit test, and applicants ages 15 to 17 generally need a parent or guardian signature
A learner permit generally must be held for at least 1 year before a junior driver's license
Before the junior license, Vermont requires 40 additional practice hours with at least 10 at night, beyond the 6 hours of behind-the-wheel driver-training instruction

During the first 3 months of a junior license, the driver is generally restricted to driving alone unless a qualified supervising adult is in the front seat; during the next 3 months, only parents and siblings may ride without that adult; after 6 months the passenger restriction ends

name: Virginia
uniqueRequirements: The test is strictly split into two parts: 10 Traffic Signs and 30 General Knowledge questions
CRITICAL: You must answer ALL 10 traffic sign questions correctly (100%) in Part 1 to proceed; missing even one sign results in automatic failure
You must score at least 80% (24/30) on Part 2 (General Knowledge) to pass
Drivers under 18 are prohibited from using a cell phone in any manner (handheld or hands-free) while driving
New 2025 Law: All passengers (front and back seat) must wear seat belts regardless of age (Christopher King Seat Belt Law)

name: Washington
uniqueRequirements: Must hold permit for 6 months before road test
50 hours of supervised driving required (10 at night)
Intermediate license holders under 18 prohibited from driving 1am–5am
No passengers under 20 (except immediate family) for first 6 months of intermediate license

name: West Virginia
uniqueRequirements: West Virginia's knowledge examination has at least 25 questions, and applicants must answer 19 correctly to pass
The Level 1 GDL instruction permit begins at age 15 and requires a vision screening, knowledge test, school eligibility certificate, and parental or guardian consent for most minors
Level 1 permit holders may drive only from 5 AM to 10 PM with a licensed driver age 21 or older in the right front seat and may have only 2 additional non-family passengers

Before the Level 2 GDL intermediate license, West Virginia generally requires 180 consecutive conviction-free days at Level 1 plus either 50 behind-the-wheel hours including 10 at night or an approved driver education course

During the first 6 months of Level 2, no non-family passengers under 20 are allowed; during the second 6 months, only 1 non-family passenger under 20 is allowed, and nighttime driving from 10 PM to 5 AM generally requires a qualifying exception or supervising adult

name: Wisconsin
uniqueRequirements: Wisconsin's permit process includes a separate 15-question highway signs test requiring 12 correct answers and a 50-question knowledge test requiring 40 correct answers
The Class D instruction permit begins at age 15 and generally requires a sponsor, a knowledge test, a highway signs test, and a vision screening
The instruction permit generally expires 18 months after issuance and requires supervision by a qualified adult with at least 2 years of licensed driving experience who holds a valid regular license

Before the probationary license, Wisconsin teens under 18 generally must hold the instruction permit 6 months violation-free, turn 16, complete driver education, and log 50 supervised hours including 10 during darkness

During the first 9 months of a probationary license or until age 18, Wisconsin generally limits the driver to 1 non-family passenger, restricts driving from midnight to 5 AM except for home, school, or work, and requires absolute sobriety

name: Wyoming
uniqueRequirements: Wyoming's regular learner permit is generally issued at ages 15 to 16, requires a written exam and vision screening, and is valid for 1 year
Permit holders generally must drive with a licensed person over age 18 in the front passenger seat, and the permit must be held at least 10 days before applying for broader driving privileges
For the intermediate permit at age 16, Wyoming generally requires 50 actual behind-the-wheel hours including 10 at night plus a driving skills test

Wyoming's intermediate permit generally allows driving only from 5 AM to 11 PM, bars more than 1 passenger under 18 who is not immediate family, and requires all occupants to wear seat belts unless a qualifying 18+ fully licensed driver is in the front seat

WYDOT public materials reviewed were clearer about graduated-license ages, hours, and restrictions than the exact scored Class C written-test question count, so the app uses a best-fit 25-question, 80% study format
